steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The key is the consequence clause: the kite goes so high that Gogol must tip his head back to see it — the word must describe an upward physical action performed on the kite.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Create’: “Create a kite within minutes” means to make one from scratch, but the kite already exists and is being sent into the air. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Congratulate’: “Congratulate a kite” is nonsensical; you congratulate a person, not an object. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Strengthen’: “Strengthen a kite” would mean making it sturdier, which has nothing to do with the kite reaching a height that forces Gogol to look upward. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Lift’: “Lift a kite into the wind” matches perfectly — the father elevates the kite until it rises so high that Gogol must crane his neck back. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
